resources.string: Search sources, speed, result layout, and the app drawer
Settings lock, AI key, and data control
Search apps, folders, settings, contacts or files
Tap to grant permission for contact results
Choose how the folder swipe animation looks
Report a bug or suggestion by e-mail

Your apps get sorted into smart folders in seconds, powered by a 3,700+ app category database. Weekly digital life reports, smart suggestions and one search box that finds everything. All your data stays on your device.
What do you gain in the first minute?
Popular apps are organized through the category database; low-confidence decisions wait for review.
Find apps, folders, settings, contacts and files from one place; sources can be disabled in Settings.
Screen time, notification load and usage rhythm are analyzed on your device.
Notification content is not read by default; search statistics never store query text.
Default launcher is selected. If you have a JSON backup from your previous phone, you can restore it now.
Restoring a backup brings category assignments, hidden state and supported settings to this device.
Fresh setup or your old layout?
If you do not have a backup, skip this step. You can restore later from Settings > About and Backup.
Backup file is empty or unreadable
This permission is required to view and organize your apps.
The launcher cannot work without this permission.
Required to show smart suggestions and recent apps accurately.
Android grants this from a separate system screen; without it, suggestions stay incomplete.
Usage data stays on your device and is never sent out.
Permission is needed to show notification counts on app icons.
Show apps you never open in a faded color?
Lets you visually tell apart apps you don\'t use.
Automatically save a JSON backup of your category assignments on each launch?
Weekly background backup â€” you won\'t lose your data.
Show the latest notification below folders and apps?
See the latest message without opening the app.
Full access is required to show real notification counts on app icons.
Android requires a separate permission for this.
ğŸ”’ All notification data stays on your device only â€” nothing leaves it.
Show swipe-up animation on the home screen?
Helps new users discover the feature.
Show a \'NEW\' badge on apps installed within the last 7 days?
Makes it easy to keep track of apps you just installed.
Show the number of apps below each folder icon?
Quickly see how many apps are in each folder.
Hide Back/Home/Recents buttons for a full-screen launcher.
The bottom of the screen is fully dedicated to content, giving you a wider view.
Pick your preferred color theme and font.
Your selected theme is applied throughout the launcher.
Review and change all your preferences on one screen.
You can change these settings later from the Settings menu.
Show clock/weather widgets on the home screen
Show your most-used apps on the home screen
Search apps, folders and settings on the home screen
See the automatic folder layout and review queue before setup finishes.
Low-confidence decisions wait for your approval; incorrect folders are not locked automatically.
Which browser would you like to set as default? Links will open in this browser.
Set AppOrganizer as your default launcher.
The home screen won\'t show without setting it as launcher.
We can group your apps by category or by manufacturer.
Setup complete â€” your apps are already organized! Smart folders, your weekly report and universal search are waiting on the home screen. Enjoy exploring!
AppOrganizer needs to see notifications to build this report. After you grant access, data is collected on your device only â€” content is never stored, just app name and time.
You turned Notification Analysis off â€” while it\'s off, no new data is collected and no report is built. If you enable it, data stays on your device; notification content is never stored.
Analysis is on, but not enough notifications have accumulated yet. After a few days of normal use, your report will appear here.
Notification Analysis is off â€” the report is not being updated.
Notification access not granted â€” the report is not being updated.
Apps that send many notifications but are rarely used â€” consider muting them.
No apps disturbing you at night ğŸ‰
No app can access sensitive permissions
No app has been granted sensitive permissions like camera, microphone, or location.
You have turned off Privacy Analysis. Turn it on to see which sensitive permissions your installed apps have â€” nothing is ever sent anywhere.
All analysis happens on your device â€” nothing is ever sent anywhere.
See which apps can access camera, microphone, location and more
Show the Privacy Analysis entry in the Reports Center
Powers categorization for the Other folder and the Weekly Report AI coach
Notification counts are recorded to feed the Notification Report (content is never stored)
See last 7 days of notification behavior â€” talkative, disturbing, distracting
%1$d apps, %2$d categories and %3$d hidden apps summarized. Most used, never opened and hide suggestions all on one screen.
Your weekly report will appear here after you use a few apps.
Grant usage access for a more accurate score and badges.
The AI coach comment requires a DeepSeek key. You can add one under Settings > Privacy & Data.
Gathering data â€” comparison ready next week
%1$d apps are disturbing you with notifications
Your notification load is calm this week
%1$d apps have not been opened in a long time
Unlocks are up %%%1$d vs last week
Unlocks are down %%%1$d vs last week
%1$d apps are distracting you with notifications
No notification access, attention stayed neutral
Your usage mix is close to your normal
%1$d apps have not been opened in a long time
No unlock data, consistency stayed neutral
Mission score applied a controlled adjustment
Digital life score %1$d, %2$d points %3$s vs last week
Digital life score %1$d, same as last week
Digital life score %1$d, gathering data for comparison
Show live weather and hourly temperatures on the clock card
Use approximate location instead of a manual city
Select a city to enable weather
Weather data could not be loaded
Move the search bar to the bottom for one-handed use
Shows the digital life score ring in the clock card
Shows the card with your digital life score and trend on the home screen
Shows a one-line personal insight in the clock card
Reserve a separate page for tasks, digital life and suggestions
Choose which page opens first when you go home
Shows the last 24 hours of usage as a mini chart under the score ring
A few apps are waiting for a folder
Shows one contextual card instead of separate task, digital life and suggestion cards; turning it off brings the separate cards back
Usage chart for the last 24 hours
Don\'t show this type of item
Short reminders and updates flowing on the home screen
Quick-fix items like your notification summary
Ongoing missions and the achievements you\'ve earned
Meaningful ups and downs in your Digital Life score
A short nudge when your weekly summary is ready
Contextual tips like forgotten apps and insights
Numbered, provable suggestions about storage, unused apps, and notification load
Briefly introduces features you haven\'t discovered yet
Storage %%%1$d full; %2$d apps unused for %3$d days take up %4$s GB
Usage permission is off — your score can\'t update
Warnings for critical issues like missing permissions
Items move to the next one on their own
Moves to the next item every %1$d seconds
When off, things like your active notification count stay hidden from the strip
Types you turned off from the strip\'s long-press menu (\"Don\'t show this type of item\") can also be turned back on here.
Digital life score, personality type and badges â€” shown in the Reports Center
When each app was last opened
Weekly report and digital life score trend
The following data will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone:
Suggest a web/Play Store search when no results are found
Open all results in a full-screen overlay when the search bar is tapped
Removes the last 3 searches shown on the empty query screen
People You Contact At This Time
Start typing to search apps, folders, contacts, files, and settings in one place.
Tap to grant Android contacts permission
Suggests frequently contacted people based on the time of day. No call log access - only actions started from the launcher are learned
Deletes the saved contact action history and resets suggestions
Off by default. Asks for Android file permission for media/download names; content is not read
Permission required â€” file names are not indexed yet
File search needs an Android permission. Without it, file results are not shown.
File names are being scanned in the background, you can keep using the app.
File indexing could not finish. You can try again.
Tap to grant Android file permission
Statistics stay on your device only; searched text is never recorded.
🏆 Your Master reward is unlocked: gold clock style + gold folder color — turn it on in Settings
You reached Master level — adds a gold accent to the clock text
Mission completion animation, 3/3 day glow, and morning summary
Keep your screen time under 3 hours today
Do not use your phone after 11 PM
Unlock your phone fewer than 30 times today
Resolve at least 1 classification decision today
Keep weekly screen time below last week
Do at least 3 useful cleanup actions this week
Give a folder an emoji or color
Start your day without social media (first 30 min)
Spend at least 30 min in Focus Mode today
Will be decided at period end
Daily/weekly missions, star rewards and the home screen personality label
See today\'s missions and your stars
Show apps installed today on the home screen and app drawer
Smart Home is ready â€” turn on cards and widgets in Settings to customize this page
When off, the app drawer uses a single menu button instead
Data is building up for comparison
Usage varies a lot week to week
Your mission progress added +%1$d to your score
Mission status affected your score by %1$d
Anonymous usage, performance, and error sharing
Share anonymous usage and health data

When enabled, Firebase Analytics sends feature usage counters, Crashlytics sends error and crash records, and Performance sends operation timings to Google. When disabled, the app sends no new telemetry to these three services; the Crashlytics automatic-collection change takes full effect on the next app start. FCM database-update notifications are independent of this switch.
Health warning codes without personal data
The Firebase connection test and health report are now under Settings > Advanced & Support > About & Backup.
This APK was built without Firebase
This build is intended only for code and CI verification. Analytics, Crashlytics, and Performance are unavailable. Install the Firebase-enabled test APK for device testing.
Firebase is disabled in this build
No connection test has been run yet.
SDK configuration verified. Dashboard visibility should be confirmed separately in the Firebase console.
Firebase configuration found. Service access will be verified by the next connection test.
The Analytics SDK does not confirm delivery. Verify the test event separately in Firebase DebugView.
Draft layout of your Home screen sections
Your unsaved changes will be discarded.
Return to the recommended Home screen layout?
Section order and visibility preferences will be reset. Folder, widget, and dock contents will not be deleted.
Drag handle for moving the section
%1$s is required and cannot be hidden
The Search Everything bar stays fixed at the top or bottom (above the dock) of the Home screen.
The dock always stays fixed at the bottom; its position can\'t change, but its apps can be reordered above.
Your screen looks small â€” keeping many sections on may require scrolling on the Home screen.
